тАЬOkay...Cut west of the Sanctum, down Mercer...тАЭ

MJтАЩs voice crackled through the comms, steady and crisp in his ear. One of those oddly calming sounds in the chaos of everything else, like the tick of a metronome when the symphony of his life was just barely holding together. And right now? Mid-swing, boots gliding above ManhattanтАЩs traffic, wind clawing at his jacketтАФPeter needed that steady.

She was helpful. Always helpful. Especially when things got messy, or loud, or wildly dangerous. And they often did. She understood how his brain moved. Too fast, too sideways, too everywhere. And she never made him feel like a burden for it. Just anchored him, without needing to say it out loud.

Which was why he was trying to be good this time.

HeтАЩd been bad at the whole boyfriend thing. Okay, maybe not bad bad. But distracted, often. Pulled in too many directions. Balancing two lives on a thread thinner than his webs. And sure, he missed things. A lot of things. But it wasnтАЩt on purpose. He wanted to be better.

MJ had broken up with him last month, and yeah, it hurt like hell. But now they were trying again. A fresh start. And he was determined not to ruin it.

тАЬCiti is right ahead of youтАЭ MJ said, cutting through the cold wind like a thread of warmth.

Peter landed lightly on the roof of the bank, knees bending with the motion, one hand brushing the gravel-dusted ledge. The siren howled in the distance, automated and indifferent, but up here it was all too still. No police lights. No chaos. Just eerie quiet and the brittle sharpness of a city holding its breath.

He crouched, crawling toward the glass edge, peering into the dark. Everything looked untouched. W
